summary_en: Union Cabinet approved Chennai Metro Phase 2 with Rs.7,425 cr from GOI, Rs.33,593 cr loans, and Rs.22,228 cr from state.
10,000 km of rural roads to be upgraded in next two years at Rs 4000 crore under CM's Village Roads Development Scheme
CM laid foundation for 60 MLD desalination plant at Rs 2,292.38 crore to meet water needs of SIPCOT and surrounding industries.
CM launched Rs 2,119.07 crore Cauvery-based combined drinking water project covering 8 town panchayats, 11 panchayat unions and 2,452 rural habitations.
Land worth Rs 2088.92 crore handed over to AAI for Coimbatore Airport expansion.
CM inaugurated Rs 769.97cr completed projects and laid foundation for Rs 1192.45cr new water and sanitation projects
Tamil Nadu's longest 10.10 km four-lane flyover from Goldwins to Uppilipalayam constructed at Rs 1791 crore inaugurated
79 new projects worth Rs 1383 crore under Phase 2 of North Chennai Development Programme and 29 completed projects inaugurated.
CM inaugurated 9,696 new urban housing units built at Rs 1,238.19 crore across Tamil Nadu under TNUHDB.
6746 multi-storey housing units to be reconstructed at Rs 1149 crore
CM inaugurated 109 km widened and upgraded Seyyur-Panayur-Vaniyambadi-Polur road at Rs 1141.23 crore with bypass roads, bridges, CCTV, bus stops.

name_en: TNUHDB Multi-storey Housing and TNHB Government Quarters
name_ta: TNUHDB அடுக்குமாடி குடியிருப்பு மற்றும் TNHB அரசு குடியிருப்பு
summary_en: CM inaugurated 4184 TNUHDB flats worth Rs 541.32cr and 1459 TNHB government quarters worth Rs 382.84cr
CM laid foundation for multi-purpose integrated bus terminal and Kuralakam building at Rs 822.70 crore at Broadway.
CM inaugurated 1046 TNHB apartments worth Rs 659.96 crore and 464 government employee rental quarters worth Rs 116.55 crore.
CM laid foundation for TIDEL Parks in Trichy (Rs 403 crore, 5.58 lakh sq ft) and Madurai (Rs 314 crore, 5.34 lakh sq ft) expected to create 12,000 jobs.

name_en: Police Quarters and Coimbatore Central Prison
summary_en: Foundation stone laid for 1118 police quarters at Rs 457.14 crore and new Coimbatore central prison at Rs 211.57 crore
Bus terminus projects announced: Koyambedu Rs 400cr, Chengalpattu Rs 97cr, Mamallapuram Rs 80cr, Mudichur Rs 27.90cr
Site inspection for proposed TIDEL Park at Mattuthavani on 5.63 acres with Rs 600 crore investment.
CM reviewed construction of world-class Kalaignar International Convention Centre being built on 37.99 acres at Rs 525 crore on ECR.
Opened Rs 424.38 crore four-lane highway, Rs 68.38 crore railway overbridge, and Rs 6.32 crore tribal housing at Ranipet
Rs 481 crore Aathupalam-Ukkadam flyover completed and ready for public use

name_en: Village Roads and Government Jobs Announcement
name_ta: கிராம சாலைகள் மற்றும் அரசு வேலைவாய்ப்பு அறிவிப்பு
summary_en: Rs 450 crore for 10,000 km village roads upgrade, 75,000 government jobs for youth

name_ta: முத்தமிழறிஞர் கலைஞர் ஒருங்கிணைந்த பேருந்து முனையம் திருச்சி
summary_en: CM inaugurated Rs 408.36 crore integrated bus terminus and unveiled Kalaignar statue

name_ta: முத்தமிழறிஞர் கலைஞர் ஒருங்கிணைந்த பேருந்து முனையம் திருச்சி
summary_en: CM inaugurated Rs 408 crore airport-standard integrated bus terminus named after Kalaignar
CM laid foundation for 27-storey Central Tower at Chennai Central metro station area with Rs 350 crore investment.
CM laid foundation for Mamallan drinking water reservoir spanning 5,161 acres at Rs 342.60 crore between Old Mamallapuram Road and ECR.
21-storey TIDEL Park on 11.41 acres with 5.57 lakh sq ft inaugurated at Rs 330 crore, creating 6,000 IT jobs.

name_ta: தமிழ்நாடு நகர்ப்புற வாழ்விட மேம்பாட்டு வாரிய குடியிருப்புகள்
summary_en: CM laid foundation for 2404 new apartments under TNUHDB at Rs 327.69 crore investment.

name_en: Fishing Harbors and Landing Centers Upgrade
summary_en: Opened Rs 153.43 crore upgraded fishing facilities and laid foundation for Rs 170 crore landing centers at Ramanathapuram

name_en: Periyar Library and Science Center Coimbatore
name_ta: தந்தை பெரியார் நூலகம் மற்றும் அறிவியல் மையம்
summary_en: Foundation stone for Periyar Library and Science Center with 1.98 lakh sq ft across 8 floors at Rs 300 crore. Expected inauguration January 2026.

name_en: Chennai City Partnership Programme - MTC Fund
name_ta: சென்னை நகர கூட்டாண்மைத் திட்டம் - MTC நிதி
summary_en: World Bank funded Viability Gap Fund of Rs.300 crore allocated for Metropolitan Transport Corporation.

Inaugurated police buildings (Rs 27.59 cr), fire stations (Rs 13.54 cr), prison buildings (Rs 60 lakh), forensic drug testing units (Rs 3.74 cr), laid foundation for Madurai Central Prison (Rs 229.20 cr)
India's first dedicated tuna fishing harbor capable of handling 7000 tonnes annually
Opened 4 fitness parks at Rs 11.46 crore and Somasundaram sports ground at Rs 3.20 crore; foundation for 20 new projects at Rs 255.60 crore

name_en: Tamil Nadu House New Delhi Construction
summary_en: CM laid foundation stone via video for new buildings at Vaigai Tamil Nadu House in New Delhi worth Rs 257 crore
Launched 120 new low-floor electric buses at Rs 207.90 crore and upgraded Rs 47.50 crore Vyasarpadi e-bus depot - first in Tamil Nadu
CM laid foundation for Rs 236 crore integrated vegetable market and unveiled Periyar statue

name_ta: இந்து சமய அறநிலையத் துறை கோயில் பணிகள்
summary_en: Rs 190.40 crore for new projects in 18 temples and Rs 42.75 crore for completed works in 15 temples.
Started 17 new temple projects (Rs 124.97 cr), renovation of 9 thousand-year-old temples (Rs 32.53 cr), inaugurated 14 completed works (Rs 51.19 cr)
CM inaugurated world-class Semmozhi Poonga on 45 acres in Coimbatore built at Rs 208.50 crore with modern amenities.
Highways Department approved Rs 208.31 crore six-lane bridge across Rajakilpakkam Lake to complete Tambaram Eastern Bypass.

name_en: Commercial Complexes by TN Housing Board
summary_en: Inaugurated 4 commercial complexes built at Rs 207.90 crore by Tamil Nadu Housing Board
Listed Rs 200 crore Chennai Corporation municipal bonds on National Stock Exchange for stormwater drain infrastructure
Industries Minister inspected 20 MLD TTRO water treatment plant under construction at Rs 182 crore to supply treated water to industrial parks in Hosur.
CM inaugurated 776 urban housing units worth Rs 147 crore near Stanley Hospital under North Chennai Development Scheme, and laid foundation for 144 new units worth Rs 31 crore.
Inaugurated 9 completed fisheries projects including new and upgraded fish landing centers, fish seed farm and training center with office building
Deputy CM inspected Rs 170 crore flyover construction over railway line
CM laid foundation for integrated multi-modal transport and commercial complex at Rs 167.09 crore at Mandaveli bus depot.
Chennai's first steel flyover connecting South Usman Road and CIT Nagar constructed at Rs 164.92 crore inaugurated
IT building inaugurated at Vilankurichi with 2.94 lakh sq ft at Rs 158.32 crore.

name_en: Coimbatore Metro Rail Land Acquisition Fund
name_ta: கோவை மெட்ரோ ரயில் நிலம் கையகப்படுத்துதல் நிதி
summary_en: Chennai Metro Rail allocated Rs 154 crore as first phase for land acquisition for Coimbatore Metro Rail project.
CM inaugurated new Kallakurichi District Collectorate complex built on 39.81 acres at Rs 139.41 crore.

name_ta: பேரறிஞர் அண்ணா கனரக சரக்கு வாகன முனையம் திருச்சி
summary_en: CM inaugurated Rs 128.94 crore heavy vehicle terminus named after Anna
Industries Minister inspected Rs 124 crore ring road construction connecting 6 major roads, covering 11.9 km

name_en: Fire and Rescue Training Academy Foundation
summary_en: Rs 21.85 crore State Training Academy foundation, Rs 97.65 crore police housing and stations, fire department buildings inaugurated
Foundation laid for 6 new projects including Kolathur Ornamental Fish Market (Rs.53.50 cr), community hall, lake bund improvements totaling Rs.115.58 crore plus Rs.5.22 cr inaugurations.
Second IT Park inaugurated at Coimbatore worth Rs 114.16 crore.
CM inspected Rs 113 crore exhibition centre with 1.63 lakh sq ft area under construction
Fund allocated for electric bus infrastructure at 6 depots and diesel bus conversion at 5 depots.
230/33 KV Gas Insulated Substation under North Chennai Development Plan constructed at Rs 110.92 crore inaugurated
CM reviewed Rs 110.92 crore 230/33 KV gas-insulated sub-station under construction in Kolathur

name_en: MS Swaminathan Wetland Green Park and Market Upgrades
name_ta: எம்.எஸ்.சுவாமிநாதன் ஈரநில பசுமை பூங்கா மற்றும் சந்தை மேம்பாடு
summary_en: CM inaugurated Rs 39.75 crore park at Porur and market upgrades, laid foundation for Rs 70.70 crore new projects
Inaugurated Perumbakkam park (Rs 6.94 cr), flyover beautification at Villivakkam, Padi, Vadapalani (Rs 11.50 cr), laid foundation for 13 CM Creative Studios among 26 projects (Rs 91.42 cr)
Modern equipment worth Rs 105.36 crore for disaster rescue operations
Flagged off 248 new BS-VI Vidiyal Payanam buses for women's free travel scheme

name_en: Police and Fire Station Buildings and Vehicles
name_ta: காவல் நிலையங்கள் மற்றும் தீயணைப்பு கட்டடங்கள், வாகனங்கள்
summary_en: CM inaugurated 96 police quarters, 2 police stations at Rs 19.8 crore; 45 fire staff quarters and 4 fire stations at Rs 21.52 crore; vehicles worth Rs 60.54 crore
Started restoration work on 63 temples that are over 1000 years old, preserving their ancient heritage

name_en: New Ration Shops and Paddy Procurement Centers
name_ta: புதிய நியாய விலைக் கடைகள் மற்றும் நெல் கொள்முதல் நிலையங்கள்
summary_en: TN government opened 1666 new ration shops and 230 paddy procurement centers worth Rs 100 crore in last 40 months.
Opened upgraded storm water drain as open and covered channel system
CM reviewed Rs 91.36 crore project to widen surplus water canal in Aynavaram-Madhavaram area

name_ta: TNSTC புதிய AC படுக்கை வசதி பேருந்துகள்
summary_en: 150 new AC sleeper buses procured for Tamil Nadu State Express Transport Corporation.

name_en: HRCE Temple Projects and College Infrastructure
name_ta: இந்து சமய அறநிலையத் துறை கோயில் திட்டங்கள்
summary_en: CM laid foundation for 24 new temple projects at Rs 79.94 crore and inaugurated 4 completed temple works at Rs 6.77 crore.
19 completed water projects including check dams, canals, dam reconstruction across 8 districts inaugurated.
169 police quarters, 2 police stations (Rs.56.26 cr), 10 fire service quarters, 7 fire stations (Rs.18.78 cr), forensic DNA lab (Rs.3.75 cr) inaugurated.
New 6-floor District Collector office on 7.67 acres at Rs 77.12 crore.
CM laid foundation stone for new Chennai Corporation Council Hall at Rs 74.70 crore in Ripon Building complex.
CM inaugurated 19 panchayat union office buildings worth Rs 71.94 crore across 10 districts
Deputy CM inspected Rs 70 crore bypass road construction work at Thiruthuraipoondi
Rs 66.78 crore project to supply additional 265 MLD water from Chembarambakkam to Chennai, benefiting 20 lakh people
CM inaugurated Rs 40.04 crore commercial complex and vegetable market, and Rs 26 crore renovated children's park at Nayinar Tank.
CM inaugurated Rs 20.53cr completed works, laid foundation for Rs 35.57cr new projects, and opened Rs 9.10cr ropeway at temples
Rs 65 crore project for dredging, wall elevation and chain fencing at Otteri Nalla Canal
17 Panchayat Union office buildings across 12 districts at Rs 64.53 crore.
CM inaugurated Rs 61.74 crore road overbridge named after freedom fighter Sankaralingam between Sivakasi and Srivilliputhur.
Inaugurated 5 circle processing godowns (Rs 23.27 cr), 3 modern paddy storage complexes (Rs 30.38 cr), 2 modern warehouses (Rs 7.20 cr)

beneficiaries: Kannigapuram residents and TNUHDB housing residents
summary_en: CM laid foundation for Rs 59.15 crore water supply project for individual buildings and TNUHDB housing in Thiruvallikeni constituency.
Kalaignar Centenary Food Testing Lab (Rs.4.66 cr), 4 storage complexes (Rs.17.04 cr), 58 paddy procurement centers (Rs.36.25 cr) inaugurated.
Inaugurated District Collector additional building (Rs 36.62 cr), 4 Taluk office buildings (Rs 17.52 cr), and 2 Revenue Inspector offices with residences (Rs 65.76 lakh)
CM inspected international standard ornamental fish trading center being built at Rs 53.50 crore in 3.93 acres at Villivakkam.
Perundalaivar Kamarajar integrated bus terminal with modern facilities and commercial complex at Rs 46.46 crore inaugurated
CM inaugurated the first glass bridge in India built over sea, connecting Thiruvalluvar statue with Vivekananda Rock, built at Rs 37 crore.
Mini TIDEL Park with 63,000 sq ft at Rs 32.50 crore for 600 IT professionals.
CM inaugurated TIDEL Neo Park in Thoothukudi with Rs 32.5 crore investment, 100% space already leased, fourth TIDEL Neo Park in the state.

name_en: Metro Water Vehicles and TWAD Vehicles
name_ta: மெட்ரோ நீர் வாகனங்கள் மற்றும் TWAD வாகனங்கள்
summary_en: CM flagged off 58 sewerage vehicles worth Rs 28.55cr and 10 TWAD vehicles worth Rs 1.05cr

name_en: Murasoli Maran Park Renovation and Infrastructure
summary_en: Rs 8.20 crore Murasoli Maran Park renovation, Rs 13.95 crore ICDS buildings, LED lights and other infrastructure
CM inspected bus terminal under construction at Rs 21.95 crore in RK Nagar constituency.
Announced Rs 12 cr water resource development and Rs 7.25 cr tourism development for Cholagangam Lake on Rajendra Chola birthday
New Kamarajar bus terminal with commercial complex and modern facilities constructed at Rs 16.30 crore
CM reopened Anna Flyover after Rs 10.85 crore renovation with LED lights on its golden jubilee
Industries Minister inspected Rs 6.06 crore new bridge construction over Pamani river benefiting multiple villages
Bridge construction connecting Mannargudi town and Serangulam panchayat across Pamani river under progress.
CM inaugurated upgraded Kolathur Lake Park built at Rs 4.98 crore under North Chennai Development Scheme.
CM inaugurated Rs 3 crore memorial with statue for freedom fighter Immanuel Sekaran in Paramakudi.
Inaugurated memorial for workers who lost lives during Parambikulam Aliyar irrigation project construction
Opened renovated Kumaramangalam lake and park
Lake improvement works in GKM Colony at Rs 1.47 crore.
CM inaugurated renovated Thiruvalluvar Traffic Island Park at Rs 44 lakh and laid foundation for Silver Jubilee Arch at Rs 1.45 crore.
Deputy CM inaugurated Kalaignar Park near Kalaignar Kottam in Thiruvarur.
CM to inaugurate flyover connecting Madurai-Sivagangai named after freedom fighter Veeramangai Velunachiyar.
New Thoothukudi Airport terminal opened with 712.72 acres land acquired by TN govt to support growing investments in southern TN
India's largest and longest desalination intake pipeline for South East Asia's largest 400 MLD desalination plant

name_en: Mudichur Omni Bus Terminus and Kalaignar Centenary Climate Park
name_ta: முடிச்சூர் ஆம்னி பேருந்து முனையம் மற்றும் கலைஞர் நூற்றாண்டு காலநிலைப் பூங்கா
summary_en: 150-bus capacity Omni bus terminus and Kalaignar Centenary Climate Park inaugurated.

name_en: Neo TIDEL Park Thanjavur - Hamly Business Solutions
summary_en: Thanjavur Neo TIDEL Park reaches 100% occupancy. Plans for Phase 2 expansion underway.
Land acquisition complete for Mannargudi ring road, construction starting
SIPCOT invited tenders for seawater desalination plant in Thoothukudi
